identificationModule.briefTitle: Modulation of Free Fatty Acids in Heart Failure Patients With Diabetes: "Effect on Left Ventricular Function"
identificationModule.officialTitle: Short Term Modulation of Circulating Free Fatty Acids in Heart Failure Patients With Type 2 Diabetes: "Effect on Myocardial Lipid Content, Left Ventricular Function and Exercise Capacity"

descriptionModule.briefSummary:
The investigators wish to investigate the the short term effect of low circulating free fatty acids in congestive heart failure patients with type 2 diabetes.

Hypothesis: Low levels of circulating free fatty acids decrease myocardial and peripheral muscle lipid content, improves cardiac performance and exercise capacity.

armGroups.label: High circulating free fatty acids
armGroups.description: using Heparin af intralipid infusion for 8 hours

armGroups.label: Low circulation free fatty acids
armGroups.description: using hyperinsulinaemic euglycemic clamp for 8 hours

interventions.name: Metabolic substrate modulation
interventions.description: for high circulation free fatty acids: Heparin (250IE/hour) + intralipid (20%, 62 ml/hour).

interventions.name: metabolic substrate modulation
interventions.description: low circulating free acids: hyperinsulinaemic euglycemic clamp (0,8 mUkg/min) with venous blood glucose at 4,5-6,5 mM.

primaryOutcomes.description:
Left ventricular systolic function (Ejection fraction, tissue velocity, Strain and strain rate).

Left ventricular diastolic funtion (E/A ratio, E/e' ratio, IVRT) Cardiac output. All parameters measured at rest and peak exercise and outcome is difference between low and high ciculating free fatty acids.

Magnetic Resonans proton spectroscopy (septal myocardial intracellular lipid content) Magnetic Resonans proton spectroscopy (Tibialis anterior muscle intracellular lipid content).

Outcome is difference between low and high ciculating free fatty acids.
Using treadmill and continues oxygen consumption measurement. Outcome is difference between low and high ciculating free fatty acids.

secondaryOutcomes.description: regional speckle tracking during rest and peak exercise. Outcome is difference between low and high ciculating free fatty acids.
distance difference between low and high levels of circulating FFA. Outcome is difference between low and high ciculating free fatty acids.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* ejection fraction at or lower than 45%
* type 2 diabetes

Exclusion Criteria:

* known s-creatinine \>220mM
* known S-alanine aminotransferase \>3 times normal upper limit
* other disabilitating conditions
* pregnancy
* insulin treatment

references.citation: Nielsen R, Norrelund H, Kampmann U, Kim WY, Ringgaard S, Schar M, Moller N, Botker HE, Wiggers H. Failing heart of patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus can adapt to extreme short-term increases in circulating lipids and does not display features of acute myocardial lipotoxicity. Circ Heart Fail. 2013 Jul;6(4):845-52. doi: 10.1161/CIRCHEARTFAILURE.113.000187. Epub 2013 Jun 3.
